summary: Large sysdolphin baselib memory-card decompilation pass centered on hsd_3AA7. The PR expanded CardState, replaced a narrow placeholder struct, added typed command/status structs, implemented several formerly stubbed card-operation routines, and improved matching across existing helpers. decomp.dev reported +1340 matched bytes, 3 new full matches, and 15 unmatched-item improvements in main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation work: implemented hsd_803AAA48 and many hsd_3AA7 card helpers, added CardOpStatus/CardCmd/HsdCmdEntry, and adjusted queue/status logic.
Expanded CardState layout and updated prototypes to use CardState instead of hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t or untyped s32 placeholders.
Updated hsd_803AC558 call site to cast/use CardState.
Updated hsd_803AC3E0 call site to cast/use CardState.

what_changed: Implemented hsd_803AAA48 as a large card operation dispatcher/state machine handling queued operations, async CARD calls, status propagation, callbacks, retries, and ring-buffer advancement.
Added local structs in hsd_3AA7.c: CardOpStatus for hsd_804D7988.status/xferred, CardCmd for queued card commands, and HsdCmdEntry plus CMD_QUEUE(base) for the command queue at base + 0x1210.

Expanded CardState in hsd_3AA7.h from a partial layout into a larger recovered layout including CARDFileInfo, file size/flag/data arrays, block maps x170/x270, banner/icon fields, CARDStat, digest, and trailing state fields.

Removed the narrow placeholder struct hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t and converted hsd_803AC3E0, hsd_803AC558, fn_803AC634, fn_803AC6B8, fn_803AD16C, fn_803ADF90, fn_803AE7F8, and fn_803B0E9C prototypes/call sites toward CardState.
Implemented or substantially decompiled functions including fn_803AC7DC, fn_803AD16C, fn_803ADE4C, fn_803ADF90, fn_803AE7F8, fn_803AF3F0, fn_803B0120, fn_803B0E9C, and fn_803B1338.
Refined existing helpers: fn_803AA790 became a full match, fn_803AC3F8 became a full match, hsd_803B2550 became a full match, and hsd_803AC558/fn_803AC6B8/fn_803ACC0C/fn_803ACFC0 improved.
Updated lbcardnew.c and hsd_3A94.c to use CardState casts instead of the deleted hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t type.
smart_moves: Recovering CardState broadly enough to cover both low-level baselib code and external call sites avoided keeping duplicate partial structs around the same object.
Modeling hsd_804D7988 as CardOpStatus exposed that the global is not just a scalar status; hsd_803AAA48 also uses an xferred field populated from CARDGetXferredBytes.
Using a CMD_QUEUE(base) macro documented the non-obvious queue offset at hsd_804D1138 + 0x1210 while keeping the decomp readable.
The implementation preserved CARD API retry behavior by retrying several CARD calls up to 10 times when they return -1, which appears repeatedly in the recovered logic.
Interrupt handling around async CARD calls was made explicit with OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts and hsd_804D799C busy-state transitions, matching the concurrency shape of the original.
fn_803AC6B8 was simplified by reusing fn_803AC634 for per-file block counts, reducing duplicated sector arithmetic and improving match percentage.
fn_803AA790 was matched by using typed queue indexing, direct callback null checks, and explicit sub-state switch cases rather than extra temporaries.
decomp_lessons: For card/save systems, a partial struct may block matching across many functions; promote it to the shared real struct once enough offsets are known, then update all dependent prototypes and call sites together.

Memory card async dispatch code often has two queues/rings: this slice shows a 128-entry operation buffer through hsd_804D1138/hsd_804D7980 and a 32-entry command queue using hsd_804D7990/hsd_804D7994.
CARD APIs that return -1 can require small retry loops in the original source; preserving those loops is important for both behavior and matching.
Status globals may be small structs rather than scalars. When a nearby word is used for CARDGetXferredBytes, recover the aggregate type instead of inventing separate globals.
Icon/banner status code should preserve CARDStat bitfield-style updates: commentAddr, iconAddr, bannerFormat, iconFormat, and iconSpeed are manipulated with masks and 2-bit shifts.
When matching queue processors, expression shape matters: array indexing and direct function-pointer checks can match better than byte-offset arithmetic plus temporary callback variables.
The evidence contains no human review comments, so lessons here come from the diff and decomp.dev report rather than reviewer guidance.
naming_conventions: Use CardState for the recovered card/save context instead of narrow placeholder names like hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t.
Use small descriptive local struct names for recovered command/status buffers: CardOpStatus, CardCmd, HsdCmdEntry, CardBufEntry, CardQueueEntry.
Keep unknown fields as x-offset names such as x0, x4, x20, x170, x270, x3B0, x460 until semantic names are proven.
Function names remained address-derived hsd_803*/fn_803* where semantics were not fully known; the PR did not introduce speculative high-level names.
Header prototypes should be upgraded from s32/UNK_PARAMS to CardState* only when call sites and field accesses support the recovered type.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: fn_803AA790 reached 100% by changing pointer math from `(u8*)&hsd_804D2348 + idx * 0x18` to typed `((CardQueueEntry*)&hsd_804D2348)[hsd_804D7990]` indexing.
The sub-state dispatch in fn_803AA790 was reshaped into explicit switch cases 0, 1/2, 3, and default -0x101, avoiding a broader conditional fallback.
Callback temporaries were removed in favor of direct `if (entry->x14 != NULL) entry->x14(...)` calls, which affected codegen shape.
fn_803AC168 changed destination computation to use `hsd_804D1148[hsd_804D7984]` instead of raw byte multiplication, suggesting hsd_804D1148 is typed as an array of 36-byte entries.
fn_803AC3F8 reached 100% with a compact three-entry loop plus zero-fill loop and byte assignments matching original size/flag packing.
Repeated OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts regions around CARDWriteAsync, CARDReadAsync, CARDCreateAsync, and CARDSetStatusAsync preserve original async-call scheduling codegen.
The decomp.dev bot reported 3 new full matches: fn_803AC3F8 (+62 bytes), hsd_803B2550 (+62 bytes), and fn_803AA790 (+26 bytes).

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 matched code increased to 67.80% (+0.03%, +1340 bytes).
New full matches: fn_803AC3F8, hsd_803B2550, and fn_803AA790 in main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.
Major unmatched improvements included fn_803AD16C to 59.23%, hsd_803AAA48 to 28.60%, fn_803B0120 to 48.53%, fn_803AF3F0 to 48.20%, fn_803AE7F8 to 41.72%, and fn_803ADF90 to 57.42%.
No human review comments or PR body were present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.c hsd_803AAA48 for additional matching progress.
Search for CardState field renames after x170/x270/x3B0/icon_format/icon_speed were better understood.
Look for PRs converting fn_803AF3F0, fn_803B0120, fn_803B0E9C, and fn_803AD16C from partial to full matches.
Search hsd_804D7988 and hsd_804D799C usage to confirm CardOpStatus and busy-state semantics.
Compare later hsd_3AA7 header revisions for cleanup of remaining s32 prototypes and unknown x fields.
